Container.Name: Gerbera
Container.Overview: Gerbera is a lightweight UPnP/DLNA media server for streaming local media on your home network.
Container.Description: Gerbera is a UPnP media server for serving audio, video, and images to DLNA/UPnP clients such as VLC. This template uses the official gerbera/gerbera Docker image, host networking for multicast discovery, a persistent config/database path at /var/run/gerbera, and a read-only media mount at /mnt/content. The official image automatically scans /mnt/content by default. Config and database: On first start Gerbera generates /var/run/gerbera/config.xml and an SQLite database in the same directory. Keep this path on persistent storage (mapped to appdata) so your settings, layout and scanned-content database survive container updates. You may also supply your own config.xml by placing it in this folder. Make sure the container user (UID/GID below) has write access to this path. Transcoding: The default image does NOT include transcoding tools. If you need on-the-fly transcoding (ffmpeg, vlc), switch the Repository to the transcoding variant by appending the -transcoding suffix to the tag, e.g. gerbera/gerbera:3.2.1-transcoding. A -debug variant is also available for troubleshooting. Customization: Custom JavaScript import scripts can be placed in /mnt/customization/js. A shell script at /mnt/customization/shell/gerbera_config.sh (if present) is executed every time Gerbera (re)creates config.xml. Do not expose Gerbera directly to the internet; it is intended for trusted LAN or VPN/Tailscale use.

Config.Name: Config / Database
Config.Description: Persistent Gerbera config (config.xml), SQLite database, and runtime files. Keep on persistent storage so settings and the scanned-content database survive updates. The container user (UID/GID) must have write access here. You can also drop your own config.xml in this folder.

Config.Name: Media Folder
Config.Description: Host path containing the media you want Gerbera to serve. Mounted read-only and automatically scanned by default. Example: /mnt/user/media

Config.Name: Customization Folder
Config.Description: Optional. Host path for customizing Gerbera. Place custom JavaScript import scripts in a 'js' subfolder (/mnt/customization/js). A shell script at 'shell/gerbera_config.sh' is run every time Gerbera (re)creates config.xml. Leave empty if not needed.

Config.Name: UID
Config.Description: Container runtime UID. On Unraid, 99 is usually nobody. Must have write access to the Config / Database path.

Config.Name: GID
Config.Description: Container runtime GID. On Unraid, 100 is usually users.

Config.Name: MariaDB Disable TLS Peer Verification
Config.Description: Optional. Only when using a MySQL/MariaDB database instead of the default SQLite. The MariaDB connector assumes SSL/TLS; set this to 1 to skip the certificate check if your database does not use SSL. Leave empty otherwise.

Config.Name: Web UI / HTTP Port
Config.Description: Gerbera HTTP/Web UI/media-serving port. With host networking, this must remain available on the Unraid host.

Config.Name: SSDP Multicast
Config.Description: UPnP SSDP discovery port. Host networking is recommended because Docker bridge networking does not support the multicast discovery behavior Gerbera needs.
